abstract: As deep learning models continue to achieve state-of-the-art results across domains such as computer vision and natural language processing, their increasing complexity has led to a dramatic rise in parameter counts, computational latency, and training resource requirements. Consequently, optimizing the efficiency of these models has become as critical as improving their predictive quality. This paper provides a comprehensive survey of the efficient deep learning landscape, spanning five core pillars: model compression, learning techniques, automation, efficient architectures, and infrastructure (including hardware and software tooling). We provide a systematic overview of seminal research in areas such as quantization, pruning, distillation, and neural architecture search. Beyond a literature review, we introduce a mental model for practitioners to navigate the trade-offs between model quality and footprint through strategies like "shrink-and-improve." We supplement this framework with an experiment-based guide and open-source code, demonstrating how these techniques can be combined to achieve significant gains in model size and latency on mobile devices. To our knowledge, this is the first survey to offer a holistic view of efficiency from high-level modeling down to hardware-specific support, equipping both researchers and practitioners with the tools to build faster, smaller, and more sustainable deep learning systems.
